name: CheckSmart
description_short: CheckSmart Tempe, Arizona — CheckSmart is a multi-service financial center offering check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, bill pay, and third-par...
description_long:
CheckSmart is a brick-and-mortar financial services chain with multiple locations throughout Ohio, including several stores across the Columbus metro area. The company operates as a retail financial services center, providing a range of cash-based and payment services to customers who need immediate financial transactions without access to traditional banking. CheckSmart has established a presence in smaller Ohio cities including Heath, Marion, Marysville, Reynoldsburg, Springfield, and Whitehall, positioning itself as a regional provider of accessible financial services.

CheckSmart's core in-store services include check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and bill payment. Customers can also obtain a Green Dot Visa Debit Card in-store, exchange unused gift cards for cash, and access loan products. On the lending side, CheckSmart operates as a facilitator rather than a direct lender — it connects Ohio customers with loan products from an unaffiliated third-party lender. The company does not originate loans itself. Customers are encouraged to schedule an appointment online before visiting to streamline the in-store process.

CheckSmart distinguishes itself through breadth of in-store services — combining check cashing, payment services, card products, and loan access in a single location. The appointment scheduling feature is a practical convenience that can reduce wait times. Store hours vary by day, with extended Friday hours (8 AM–8 PM) and limited Sunday hours (11 AM–5 PM). Required documents for loan applications are clearly communicated upfront: government-issued ID, proof of income or employment, a working phone number, a valid email address, and proof of address.

For consumers, the most important caveat is that CheckSmart does not lend directly — it acts as a referral intermediary for an unnamed third-party lender in Ohio. This means loan terms, rates, and approval decisions are controlled by that external party, not CheckSmart. No rate information, loan amounts, or APR disclosures are available on the location page. Customers seeking transparent cost comparisons before visiting will find limited information. CheckSmart is best suited for consumers who need a one-stop physical location for financial services like check cashing and money orders, or who want in-person assistance navigating a short-term loan application.
pros: Multiple financial services available under one roof — check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and bill pay
Online appointment scheduling available to reduce in-store wait times
Green Dot Visa Debit Card obtainable in-store — useful for unbanked customers
Gift card exchange for cash offered in-store
Extended Friday hours (8 AM–8 PM) compared to standard days
Multiple Columbus-area locations plus regional Ohio coverage
Clear pre-visit document checklist provided on the website
cons: CheckSmart is not a direct lender — loans come from an unnamed third-party lender, so terms and rates are not controlled by CheckSmart
No loan rate, APR, or fee information disclosed on the location page
In-person only model — no indication of fully online loan applications
Ohio-only footprint limits accessibility to out-of-state residents
Shortened Sunday hours (11 AM–5 PM) may be inconvenient for some customers
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked Ohio residents who need in-person check cashing or money services
Consumers who need multiple financial services — cashing, bill pay, wire transfer — in a single visit
People who prefer face-to-face assistance when applying for a short-term loan
Customers looking to exchange gift cards for cash or obtain a prepaid debit card
diagnosis: CheckSmart is best for Ohio residents who need a physical location for check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, or bill pay — potentially combined with a short-term loan referral. The primary caveat is that CheckSmart does not lend directly; loan terms and rates are set by an unaffiliated third party and are not disclosed upfront, so cost-conscious borrowers should ask about rates before committing.
services: Green Dot Visa Debit Card (in-store issuance)
